title: Recruiting and supporting a migrant to apply for an AEWV, GWSV or PSV: process steps :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Recruiting and supporting a migrant to apply for an AEWV, GWSV or PSV: process steps
Follow these steps to support a migrant to apply for an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV), or a seasonal AEWV visa – a Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) or Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV).

## How to recruit and support a migrant worker
There are steps you need to take to support a migrant worker to apply for an AEWV, or seasonal AEWV (GWSV or PSV).
- 1 Step 1: Check you have employer accreditation and a job check If we have approved your applications for AEWV employer accreditation and a job check, we send you a (also called a unique link).

### Step 1: Check you have employer accreditation and a job check
If we have approved your applications for AEWV employer accreditation and a job check, we send you a (also called a unique link).
- 2 Step 2: Recruit a migrant worker To recruit a migrant worker, the job offered must match the job that was in your approved job check application. Find information about how to recruit a migrant on our website. Hiring people from overseas You must pay all recruitment costs If you use a recruiter, you are responsible for paying all recruitment costs in and outside New Zealand. You must not pass these costs on to any migrants that you hire. These can include: advertising costs recruitment agency fees employer accreditation fees and job check application fees trade testing equipment, where the tools belong to you, and training and induction costs. You must not charge fees outside New Zealand that would be illegal if charged in New Zealand, including: payment to secure a job bonding agreements illegally binding workers to a business deductions that are unreasonable or not agreed in writing.

### Step 2: Recruit a migrant worker
To recruit a migrant worker, the job offered must match the job that was in your approved job check application.
Find information about how to recruit a migrant on our website.
Hiring people from overseas

### You must pay all recruitment costs
If you use a recruiter, you are responsible for paying all recruitment costs in and outside New Zealand. You must not pass these costs on to any migrants that you hire.
- recruitment agency fees
- employer accreditation fees and job check application fees
- equipment, where the tools belong to you, and
- training and induction costs.
You must not charge fees outside New Zealand that would be illegal if charged in New Zealand, including:
- payment to secure a job
- bonding agreements illegally binding workers to a business
- deductions that are unreasonable or not agreed in writing.
- 3 Step 3: Check the migrant is suitably qualified Before you ask a migrant to apply for a visa, you must check they: meet the skill requirements have the skills and experience listed in the job advertisement. Check if a migrant worker is suitably qualified for an AEWV Advertising the job before your job check If you use a third party to recruit, you must check they are confirming each applicant is suitably qualified and meets the AEWV skill requirements. You must keep records of the steps you or the third party took to check each applicant. Seasonal AEWV workers (GWSV and PSV) There are different skill requirements if you are hiring migrants on a GWSV or PSV. For a comparison of the requirements: Hiring migrants for seasonal peaks

### Step 3: Check the migrant is suitably qualified
Before you ask a migrant to apply for a visa, you must check they:
- meet the skill requirements
- have the skills and experience listed in the job advertisement.
Check if a migrant worker is suitably qualified for an AEWV
Advertising the job before your job check
If you use a third party to recruit, you must check they are confirming each applicant is suitably qualified and meets the AEWV skill requirements.
You must keep records of the steps you or the third party took to check each applicant.

#### Seasonal AEWV workers (GWSV and PSV)
There are different skill requirements if you are hiring migrants on a GWSV or PSV.
For a comparison of the requirements:
Hiring migrants for seasonal peaks
- 4 Step 4: Support the migrant's visa application How you ask a migrant to apply depends on if they: are applying for a new AEWV, GWSV or PSV, or already have an AEWV, GWSV or PSV and need to update their visa conditions. If they are applying for a new regular AEWV or seasonal AEWV (GWSV or PSV) You need to support the migrant worker's visa application. A migrant worker must apply for their AEWV, GWSV or PSV while your job check approval is valid – it will expire 6 months from the date it was approved. Reusing or resending AEWV job tokens and transferring AEWV workers Note You must give the visa applicant a copy of their employment agreement and a copy of the signed employment offer before they begin their application. To ask a migrant to apply for an AEWV, GWSV or PSV, follow these steps. Log into Immigration Online using RealMe®. Log in with RealMe® Go to your 'Approved jobs' — these are listed in the 'Approved jobs' section on the 'Accredited organisation', 'Employer accreditation certificate', and 'Job check' summary pages. In the table, go to the line listing the migrant you want to support. In the Options drop-down list, choose 'Send request'. Enter the migrant's full name and email address. Submit the request. The migrant will receive an email with a — this is a unique link. They must open the link and accept the request. The migrant can log in to Immigration Online and apply for an AEWV. Their application will include information about the job that you provided for the job check. We process the migrant's application — we contact them if we need more information. We email the migrant with our decision. Note Check our wait times page to find up-to-date information on how long we are taking to process applications. Employer accreditation, job check and AEWV wait times If their application is declined If we decline the migrant's application but they are eligible to have their application reconsidered and apply to do so, you cannot resend the offer to another migrant until: we have reconsidered the application and made a decision, or you tell us you have withdrawn your offer of employment from the applicant. If they already have an AEWV, GWSV or PSV The worker must apply for a Job Change (variation of conditions) to update the employer listed in their visa conditions, or change their work location. You do not need to invite them to apply using Immigration Online. Apply for a Job Change or a variation of conditions if you have a work visa You must give the worker your job check approval number. This is a 10-character number that begins with JC. It can be found at the top of your job check approval letter next to the application number. We will use this number to check you have a valid job check for this job. The worker will need to upload evidence of their job offer when they apply for a Job Change.

### Step 4: Support the migrant's visa application
How you ask a migrant to apply depends on if they:
- are applying for a new AEWV, GWSV or PSV, or
- already have an AEWV, GWSV or PSV and need to update their visa conditions.

### If they are applying for a new regular AEWV or seasonal AEWV (GWSV or PSV)
You need to support the migrant worker's visa application.
A migrant worker must apply for their AEWV, GWSV or PSV while your job check approval is valid – it will expire 6 months from the date it was approved.
Reusing or resending AEWV job tokens and transferring AEWV workers
You must give the visa applicant a copy of their employment agreement and a copy of the signed employment offer before they begin their application.
To ask a migrant to apply for an AEWV, GWSV or PSV, follow these steps.
- Log into Immigration Online using RealMe®. Log in with RealMe®
- Go to your 'Approved jobs' — these are listed in the 'Approved jobs' section on the 'Accredited organisation', 'Employer accreditation certificate', and 'Job check' summary pages.
- In the table, go to the line listing the migrant you want to support.
- In the Options drop-down list, choose 'Send request'.
- Enter the migrant's full name and email address.
- The migrant will receive an email with a — this is a unique link. They must open the link and accept the request.
- The migrant can log in to Immigration Online and apply for an AEWV. Their application will include information about the job that you provided for the job check.
- We process the migrant's application — we contact them if we need more information.
- We email the migrant with our decision.
Check our wait times page to find up-to-date information on how long we are taking to process applications.
Employer accreditation, job check and AEWV wait times

### If their application is declined
If we decline the migrant's application but they are eligible to have their application reconsidered and apply to do so, you cannot resend the offer to another migrant until:
- we have reconsidered the application and made a decision, or
- you tell us you have withdrawn your offer of employment from the applicant.

### If they already have an AEWV, GWSV or PSV
The worker must apply for a Job Change (variation of conditions) to update the employer listed in their visa conditions, or change their work location. You do not need to invite them to apply using Immigration Online.
Apply for a Job Change or a variation of conditions if you have a work visa
- You must give the worker your job check approval number. This is a 10-character number that begins with JC. It can be found at the top of your job check approval letter next to the application number. We will use this number to check you have a valid job check for this job.
- The worker will need to upload evidence of their job offer when they apply for a Job Change.
- 5 Step 5: Support workers to settle You must commit to giving your workers specific information about working and living in New Zealand. This includes community services in their local area. You need to complete these settlement activities within 1 month from the day the worker starts their employment contract. If you have an AEWV accreditation and you are placing migrants with a there are extra requirements you must meet. Accredited employer requirements to help workers settle in New Zealand

### Step 5: Support workers to settle
You must commit to giving your workers specific information about working and living in New Zealand. This includes community services in their local area.
You need to complete these settlement activities within 1 month from the day the worker starts their employment contract.
If you have an AEWV accreditation and you are placing migrants with a there are extra requirements you must meet.
Accredited employer requirements to help workers settle in New Zealand
You must notify Immigration New Zealand if:
- a worker stops working for you
- the key people in your organisation change
- your business is going through a restructure, liquidation or bankruptcy.
If things change for AEWV accredited employers

### Meeting your requirements
We check that you have met your obligations when you renew your accreditation. We may also carry out checks during your accreditation.
Meeting your AEWV accredited employer requirements

## When a worker's visa is nearing expiry
There is a limit to how long a worker can spend in New Zealand.
For a regular AEWV, the total amount of time someone can stay in New Zealand (also called ) on 1 or more AEWVs can be from 3 to 5 years and depends on:
- when the AEWV worker applied for their first AEWV
- the skill level of their job on the Australian and New Zealand Standard Classification of Occupations (ANZSCO) list or National Occupation List (NOL), and
- how much they are paid.
If your AEWV worker has reached the total time they can stay in New Zealand, they cannot apply for another AEWV until they have spent at least 12 months outside New Zealand. They may be able to apply for another type of visa, such as a resident visa, if they meet all the requirements.
How long you can stay on an AEWV
New Zealand skilled residence pathways
If they have not reached the total time they can stay in New Zealand, you may be able to support them to apply for another AEWV.
You may need to apply for another job check, including advertising for suitable New Zealand workers first. You can reuse a job check number to support an application for an AEWV where:
- the applicant already holds an AEWV
- the application for their current AEWV was submitted on or before 9 March 2025
- the applicant is applying for another AEWV to continue working in the same job as shown on their current AEWV (the job that was listed in the previous job check), and
- they meet all the requirements.
Reusing or resending AEWV job tokens and transferring AEWV workers

### When a seasonal AEWV worker's visa is expiring
There are different requirements for workers on seasonal AEWV visas when they are due to expire – the Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) or Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV).
If a worker who holds a PSV for 7 months is nearing the end of their visa (or has had multiple PSVs), they can apply for another PSV. They must leave the country for 4 months before the visa can be granted.
If a worker who holds a GWSV is nearing the end of their visa, they can apply for another GWSV. They must have met the visa condition that requires they spend at least 3 months outside New Zealand during each of the 12 months they held their previous GWSV.
Hiring migrants for seasonal peaks
Our employer helpline is open between 08:00 and 18:00, Monday to Friday.
Freephone from New Zealand landlines: 0508 558 855
- Check if a migrant is suitably qualified for an AEWV Before you ask a migrant to apply for an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) you must make sure they are suitably qualified and meet your requirements for the job.

## Check if a migrant is suitably qualified for an AEWV
Before you ask a migrant to apply for an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) you must make sure they are suitably qualified and meet your requirements for the job.
- Accredited employer requirements to help settle workers in New Zealand Giving your workers information about working and living in New Zealand is a requirement of being an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) accredited employer.

## Accredited employer requirements to help settle workers in New Zealand
Giving your workers information about working and living in New Zealand is a requirement of being an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) accredited employer.

## Reusing or resending AEWV job tokens and transferring AEWV workers
Find out if you can support your Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) worker to apply for another AEWV, resend an unused job token to another migrant, or transfer an AEWV worker due to business sale or restructure.
